query: Fare condition revenue summary: first use the describe_table tool to inspect the ticket_flights table and verify the amount and fare_conditions columns, then use the execute_query tool to compute total revenue grouped by fare condition.

Ticket-to-boarding-pass join audit: first use the get_foreign_keys tool to check how ticket_flights and boarding_passes are joined, then use the execute_query tool to determine how many ticket_flights records have a corresponding boarding pass.

Largest aircraft by seats: first use the list_tables tool to see what tables are available, then use the describe_table tool to review the seats and aircrafts_data table columns, then use the execute_query tool to determine which aircraft model has the most seats.
Top flight routes: first use the find_relationships tool to check how the flights table connects to airport data, then use the execute_query tool to identify the 5 busiest routes by flight count.
Flight status summary: first use the describe_table tool to inspect the flights table and its status column, then use the execute_query tool to get a count of flights for each status value.
Multi-ticket booking detection: first use the get_foreign_keys tool to check how tickets link to bookings, then use the execute_query tool to identify bookings with more than 3 associated tickets.
Average tickets per booking: first use the find_relationships tool to check how tickets and bookings are related, then use the execute_query tool to compute the average number of tickets per booking.

Airports_data table deep inspection: first use the list_schemas tool to list available schemas, then use the list_tables tool to confirm which tables are present, then use the describe_table tool to retrieve the full column structure of airports_data.
Flight cancellation percentage: first use the get_row_count tool to get the total number of flights, then use the execute_query tool to compute what percentage of flights were cancelled.

Aircraft fleet with range and seats: first use the get_row_count tool to check how many aircraft models exist in aircrafts_data, then if under 15, use the execute_query tool to list each aircraft with its range and seat count.

Highest revenue month: first use the get_row_count tool to verify the bookings table has over 200,000 records, then use the execute_query tool to determine which month generated the highest total booking revenue.
Most frequent passengers: first use the get_row_count tool to verify total ticket count exceeds 300,000, then use the execute_query tool to identify the 5 passengers with the most tickets.

Business class traveler count: first use the list_schemas tool to check available schemas, then use the list_tables tool to identify relevant tables, then use the execute_query tool to count the total number of passengers who flew Business class.

Mean flight duration for completed flights: first use the find_relationships tool to explore the flights table relationships, then use the describe_table tool to verify the actual_departure and actual_arrival columns exist, then use the execute_query tool to calculate average flight duration in minutes for arrived flights.

Passengers with most bookings: first use the list_tables tool to identify available tables, then use the describe_table tool to review the tickets and bookings table columns, then use the get_row_count tool to check total ticket count, then use the execute_query tool to find the top 5 passengers by booking count.

Aircraft with most boarding passes: first use the get_foreign_keys tool to trace how boarding_passes connects to ticket_flights and flights, then use the describe_table tool to review the table structures involved, then use the find_relationships tool to verify the full relationship chain to aircraft, then use the execute_query tool to determine which aircraft type has the most boarding passes.

Busiest departure city: first use the list_schemas tool to list available schemas, then use the describe_table tool to examine the flights and airports_data tables to see how departure airports map to cities, then use the execute_query tool to find the departure city with the most flights.

Routes per aircraft model: first use the describe_table tool to check the structure of the routes or flights table for aircraft_code mapping, then use the execute_query tool to count how many distinct routes each aircraft model serves.

Top departure airport by volume: first use the get_row_count tool to confirm there are more than 30,000 flights, then use the execute_query tool to identify the busiest departure airport and its flight count.

Passengers with highest booking value: first use the get_foreign_keys tool to understand how tickets relate to bookings, then use the describe_table tool to review the tickets and bookings columns, then use the execute_query tool to find passengers whose total booking value exceeds 1,000,000.
